title: Rain in an Unknown City
content: Heavy rain followed Sophia throughout her first day in the city. Umbrellas filled sidewalks, reflections covered streets, and clouds hid the skyline almost completely. Many tourists rushed indoors, disappointed by the weather. Sophia initially felt the same. Gradually, however, she began noticing details that sunny conditions might have concealed. Rain changed the colors of buildings, softened sounds, and created a slower pace throughout the city. Cafés became warm refuges where conversations lasted longer and strangers lingered over coffee. By evening, Sophia no longer viewed the weather as an obstacle. Instead, it had shaped a version of the city that felt intimate, atmospheric, and uniquely memorable.
